beg.desc: C++ programs use #include and std::cout for output. Namespaces prevent name collisions.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Understanding C++ program structure.

int.desc: Use custom namespaces and using declarations for organized code.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Organizing code into logical modules.

pro.desc: Use inline namespaces for API versioning and anonymous namespaces for internal linkage.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: API versioning in shared libraries.

beg.desc: C++ is strongly typed. Use auto for type inference from initializers.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Type-safe variable declarations.

int.desc: Use structured bindings and auto with references for concise code.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Iterating maps with structured bindings.

pro.desc: Use decltype, decltype(auto), and CTAD for advanced type deduction.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Generic library design with perfect forwarding.

beg.desc: References are aliases for existing variables. Pointers store memory addresses.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Modifying variables through function parameters.

int.desc: Use const references to avoid copies and understand lvalue vs rvalue references.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Efficient parameter passing and move semantics.

pro.desc: Use std::reference_wrapper and forwarding references for generic code.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Sorting references without copying objects.

beg.desc: std::string provides safe, dynamic strings with many useful methods.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Text processing and formatting.

int.desc: Use string_view for non-owning references and string searching methods.
int.usecase: Real-world use: URL parsing and string searching.

pro.desc: Use std::format (C++20) and constexpr strings for compile-time string processing.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Type-safe formatting replacing printf and iostream.

beg.desc: std::vector is a dynamic array that grows automatically.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamic lists of elements.

int.desc: Use vector algorithms, reserve, and emplace_back for efficient operations.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Sorting, searching, and filtering data.

pro.desc: Use std::span for non-owning array views and custom allocators for performance.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Generic algorithms that work with any contiguous container.

beg.desc: Use if/else and switch for decision making in C++.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Grade calculation.

int.desc: Use init-statements in if/switch (C++17) for scoped variables.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Scoped resource lookup with if-init.

pro.desc: Use constexpr if for compile-time branching in templates.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Type-safe serialization with compile-time dispatch.

beg.desc: C++ supports for, while, and range-based for loops.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Iterating through collections.

int.desc: Use algorithms instead of manual loops for cleaner code.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Functional-style data processing.

pro.desc: Use ranges (C++20) for composable, lazy data pipelines.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Lazy evaluation pipelines for large datasets.

beg.desc: C++ supports function overloading — same name, different parameters.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Unified interfaces for different types.

int.desc: Use default parameters, constexpr functions, and function templates.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Compile-time computation and generic utilities.

pro.desc: Use concepts (C++20) to constrain template parameters for clear error messages.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Constrained generic APIs with clear compile errors.

beg.desc: Classes encapsulate data and behavior together.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Object-oriented modeling.

int.desc: Use access modifiers, member initializer lists, and const methods.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Encapsulated financial objects.

pro.desc: Use the Rule of Five and PIMPL idiom for proper resource management.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: ABI stability and compile-time firewall with PIMPL.

beg.desc: Constructors initialize objects. Destructors clean up when objects are destroyed.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: RAII — automatic resource management.

int.desc: Use delegating constructors and explicit keyword for safe initialization.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Flexible constructors with sensible defaults.

pro.desc: Implement copy/move constructors with noexcept for optimal performance.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Efficient resource transfer with move semantics.

beg.desc: Inheritance enables code reuse. Virtual enables polymorphism.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Polymorphic shape hierarchies.

int.desc: Use pure virtual functions and interface classes for abstract types.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Pluggable logging systems.

pro.desc: Use CRTP (Curiously Recurring Template Pattern) for static polymorphism.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Static polymorphism for zero-overhead abstractions.

beg.desc: Polymorphism lets different types be used through a common interface.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Processing heterogeneous collections.

int.desc: Use std::variant as a type-safe union alternative to virtual dispatch.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Closed-set polymorphism without virtual dispatch overhead.

pro.desc: Use type erasure for runtime polymorphism without inheritance.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Sean Parent's type erasure pattern (used in Adobe libraries).

beg.desc: Overload operators to make custom types work with +, -, ==, <<, etc.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Math vector operations with natural syntax.

int.desc: Implement comparison operators using the spaceship operator (C++20).
int.usecase: Real-world use: Automatic comparison operators for version types.

pro.desc: Use user-defined literals for domain-specific numeric types.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Unit-safe physical quantity types.

beg.desc: Templates create generic functions and classes that work with any type.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Generic utility functions.

int.desc: Use class templates and template specialization for type-specific behavior.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Type-safe, fixed-capacity containers.

pro.desc: Use variadic templates and fold expressions for flexible generic APIs.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Variadic print/log functions and tuple operations.

beg.desc: The STL provides vector, map, set, deque, and more containers.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Organized data storage.

int.desc: Use unordered containers for O(1) average-case lookup performance.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Word frequency counting with hash maps.

pro.desc: Use std::flat_map (C++23) and node-based container operations.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Zero-copy map node transfer and merging.

beg.desc: Iterators connect containers to algorithms for generic data processing.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Sorting and searching with STL algorithms.

int.desc: Use transform, accumulate, and copy_if for functional data processing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Filter and transform pipelines.

pro.desc: Write custom iterators for domain-specific sequence types.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Custom sequence generators compatible with STL.

beg.desc: Smart pointers automatically manage memory. unique_ptr for sole ownership.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Automatic memory management without leaks.

int.desc: Use shared_ptr for shared ownership and weak_ptr to break cycles.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Doubly-linked structures without memory leaks.

pro.desc: Use custom deleters and make_unique with arrays for specialized resource management.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: RAII wrappers for C library resources.

beg.desc: Move semantics transfer ownership of resources instead of copying them.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Efficient transfer of large objects.

int.desc: Implement move constructors for efficient container operations.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Efficient container population without copies.

pro.desc: Use perfect forwarding and std::forward for generic factory functions.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Perfect forwarding in factory functions.

beg.desc: C++ uses try/catch/throw for exception handling.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Handling runtime errors gracefully.

int.desc: Create custom exception hierarchies for domain-specific error handling.
int.usecase: Real-world use: HTTP-style error codes in web servers.

pro.desc: Use noexcept specifications and std::expected (C++23) for error handling without exceptions.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Monadic error handling without exception overhead.

beg.desc: C++ uses ifstream and ofstream for file reading and writing.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Reading and writing text files.

int.desc: Use stringstream for in-memory string formatting and parsing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: CSV parsing and string splitting.

pro.desc: Use std::filesystem (C++17) for cross-platform file system operations.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Cross-platform directory traversal and file management.

beg.desc: Lambdas are anonymous functions you can define inline.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Inline comparators and callbacks.

int.desc: Use capture clauses to access variables from the enclosing scope.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Stateful callbacks and filtered counting.

pro.desc: Use generic lambdas and immediately-invoked lambdas for complex initialization.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Complex const initialization with IIFE pattern.

beg.desc: std::thread runs functions concurrently in separate threads.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Parallel task execution.

int.desc: Use std::async for easy async tasks with futures.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Parallel sum computation.

pro.desc: Use jthread (C++20) with stop tokens for cooperative cancellation.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Cooperative thread cancellation in server workers.

beg.desc: Mutexes protect shared data from race conditions in multi-threaded code.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Thread-safe counter updates.

int.desc: Use std::atomic for lock-free operations and std::scoped_lock for multi-mutex locking.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Lock-free counters and statistics.

pro.desc: Use condition variables and std::latch for thread synchronization.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Go-style channels for thread communication.

beg.desc: constexpr functions can be evaluated at compile time for zero-runtime cost.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Compile-time computation with zero runtime cost.

int.desc: Use constexpr arrays and if constexpr for compile-time data structures.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Lookup tables computed at compile time.

pro.desc: Use consteval (C++20) for guaranteed compile-time evaluation and constant-initialized globals.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Compile-time string hashing for efficient dispatching.

beg.desc: Design patterns solve common programming problems in reusable ways.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Singleton pattern for global services.

int.desc: Use the Observer pattern for event-driven architectures.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Event systems in UI frameworks.

pro.desc: Use the Strategy pattern with std::function for runtime-configurable algorithms.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Configurable sorting strategies in data grids.
